title: Mykonos 5-Day Beach Getaway
metaTitle: Mykonos 5-Day Beach Getaway | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 5-day trip to Mykonos, Greece: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Delos Archaeological Site & Museum and Mykonos Folklore Museum. Prices from $1646 per person (estimate).
highlights: VIP cabana at Nammos or Scorpios
overview: Spend 5 days discovering Mykonos, Greece — Mediterranean island climate with hot, dry summers (highs around 29°C) tempered by the strong "meltemi" north winds, and mild winters (highs around 15°C) when much of the island closes down. Late May to June and September offer warm weather and lively beach clubs without the peak July-August crowds and prices. This itinerary blends Delos Archaeological Site & Museum, Mykonos Folklore Museum, Little Venice with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
4 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Delos Archaeological Site & Museum, Mykonos Folklore Museum, Little Venice
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Mykonos
itinerary.description: Land at Mykonos Island National Airport (JMK) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Delos Archaeological Site & Museum — UNESCO-listed sacred island reached by short ferry. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Mykonos: Mykonos Folklore Museum & Aegean Maritime Museum
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Mykonos Folklore Museum — Traditional island life and maritime exhibits. In the afternoon, continue to Aegean Maritime Museum — Nautical history, lighthouse models and old maps. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Mykonos: Scorpios Beach Club day pass & Nammos Beach Club sunbeds
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Scorpios Beach Club day pass — Iconic sunset beach club with DJ sets. In the afternoon, continue to Nammos Beach Club sunbeds — Upscale Psarou Beach club favored by celebrities. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Delos archaeological island
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Delos archaeological island, a popular side trip from Mykonos. Return to Mykonos in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Delos archaeological island

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Rhenia & Delos boat cruise or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to JMK for your departure flight.

travelTips: Book beach club sunbeds well in advance for July-August weekends.
Most of the island effectively shuts down November through March.
Delos ferries run mostly in the morning with limited afternoon return slots — book ahead.
Renting an ATV is often more practical than taxis for reaching quieter beaches.
Reserve Little Venice dinner tables days ahead in peak season.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Lightweight breathable clothing for hot, windy summer days
A stylish cover-up for beach club dinners
Sturdy sandals for Chora's uneven cobblestones
A windbreaker for the strong meltemi winds
Cash for smaller tavernas that skip card payments
thingsToDo: Windsurfing and kitesurfing at Ftelia Beach
thingsToAvoid: Beach club minimum-spend surprises — confirm sunbed or table minimums before sitting down.
Unlicensed taxi drivers at the port or airport — use the official taxi rank.
Assuming ferries run on schedule — Aegean sailings are often delayed by wind.
Walking Chora's uneven steps in flip-flops.
Visiting November-March expecting restaurants and hotels to be open.
topRestaurants: Bill & Coo Restaurant — Fine Dining Greek ($$$$)
hiddenGems: Delos ruins beyond the main temple path
familyActivities: Boat trip to Delos for history-curious kids
Family-friendly beach clubs with shallow sections
adventureActivities: Windsurfing and kitesurfing at Ftelia Beach
Boat excursions to Rhenia Island snorkeling spots
luxuryExperiences: VIP cabana at Nammos or Scorpios
Private yacht charter around the Cyclades
Sunset catamaran cruise with a private chef
Cliffside villa stay with an infinity pool
honeymoonExperiences: Couples spa treatment at a five-star resort
Secluded beach day at Agrari or Fokos
budgetExperiences: Free wander through Chora and Little Venice
Public bus to Paradise or Platys Gialos beaches
Free sunset watching by the windmills

festivalCalendar.name: Feast of Agios Panteleimon
festivalCalendar.timing: July 27, religious celebration with village feasts

localEtiquette: Topless or nude sunbathing is only accepted at specific designated beach areas, not everywhere.
Dress modestly when entering Panagia Paraportiani or other island churches.
Reserve beach club sunbeds and tables in advance during July-August.
Keep noise down late at night in residential Chora alleys near guesthouses.
Many restaurants add a discretionary service charge; a small extra tip for good service is still appreciated.
localTransportGuide: Mykonos runs a public KTEL bus network from Chora to the main beaches (Paradise, Platys Gialos, Elia) at low cost, but most visitors rent an ATV or quad bike or use hotel shuttles and taxis since routes to quieter beaches are limited. Roads are narrow and hilly, and taxis are scarce in peak season, so book ahead where possible.
bestTimeToVisit: Late May to June and September offer warm weather and lively beach clubs without the peak July-August crowds and prices.
weather: Mediterranean island climate with hot, dry summers (highs around 29°C) tempered by the strong "meltemi" north winds, and mild winters (highs around 15°C) when much of the island closes down.
visaInformation: Schengen area — visa-free entry for US, UK, Canadian, Australian and EU passport holders for stays up to 90 days within a 180-day period. Other nationalities, including Indian passport holders, need a Schengen visa arranged in advance.
averageFlightTime: ~9.8h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Mykonos?
faqs.answer: Schengen area — visa-free entry for US, UK, Canadian, Australian and EU passport holders for stays up to 90 days within a 180-day period. Other nationalities, including Indian passport holders, need a Schengen visa arranged in advance.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Mykonos?
faqs.answer: Late May to June and September offer warm weather and lively beach clubs without the peak July-August crowds and prices. Mediterranean island climate with hot, dry summers (highs around 29°C) tempered by the strong "meltemi" north winds, and mild winters (highs around 15°C) when much of the island closes down.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Mykonos safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Mykonos is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Mykonos?
faqs.answer: The local currency is EUR. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: Yes — Mykonos is one of our most-booked honeymoon destinations, especially the Sunset dinner in Little Venice experience.

reviews.author: Chen Ibrahim
reviews.title: So many hidden gems included
reviews.body: Booked this as a solo trip and it was well paced. Delos Archaeological Site & Museum in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Anna Kowalski
reviews.title: A little rushed but still wonderful
reviews.body: Mykonos is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Windmills of Kato Mili. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Elena Kowalski
reviews.title: Five stars, will return
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Ano Mera village and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Anna Larsen
reviews.title: Solid mid-range package
reviews.body: Mykonos is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Windmills of Kato Mili. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Elena Sharma
reviews.title: Great value for money
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Scorpios Beach Club day pass and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Emma van Dijk
reviews.title: Luxury without the hassle
reviews.body: Our trip to Mykonos was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Mykonos Town (Chora) alleys was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Omar Rossi
reviews.title: Perfect for our honeymoon
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Nammos Beach Club sunbeds and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Hiroshi Nguyen
reviews.title: Great for families with kids
reviews.body: Armenistis Lighthouse alone was worth the trip, but combined with Mykonos Folklore Museum this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Tom Larsen
